title: First completed film
description: Finish the studio's first feature and establish a filmography.
note: The studio now has a finished calling card.

title: First profitable film
description: Complete a film while maintaining a healthy studio cash position.
note: Investor confidence creates a modest financing bonus.

title: First festival selection
description: Build enough prestige around a completed film to enter festival circulation.
note: Festival visibility strengthens the studio's cultural profile.

title: First award win
description: Turn acclaimed work into the studio's first award-era identity.
note: The award becomes a permanent part of studio positioning.

title: Recognized studio
note: Recognition improves access to development partners.

title: Established studio
note: The studio can negotiate from a position of strength.

title: Prestige contender
note: Curators and critics now follow the studio's slate.

title: International prestige house
note: The studio can package ambitious prestige projects.

title: Three-film slate
note: A credible catalogue reduces dependence on a single release.

title: Genre identity formed
description: Become visibly associated with genre filmmaking.
note: A coherent genre identity improves audience expectations.

title: International breakthrough
description: Build an international identity backed by reputation and prestige.
note: Foreign partners and sales agents now seek out the studio.

title: Studio survives year one
description: Finish a debut film and retain positive cash through the opening year.
note: Operational resilience earns trust from future collaborators.
